Heart Disease Analysis

2025-08-20

R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document for heart disease analysis using logistic regression models. When you click the Knit button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document.

Load Libraries and Data

# Install necessary packages if not already installed
# install.packages(c("missForest", "MASS", "car", "brglm", "ggplot2", "pROC", "caret", "lattice"))

# Load libraries
library(missForest)
## Warning: package 'missForest' was built under R version 4.4.3
library(MASS)
library(car)
## Loading required package: carData
library(brglm)  # For handling separation in logistic regression
## Warning: package 'brglm' was built under R version 4.4.3
## Loading required package: profileModel
## Warning: package 'profileModel' was built under R version 4.4.3
## 'brglm' will gradually be superseded by the 'brglm2' R package (https://cran.r-project.org/package=brglm2), which provides utilities for mean and median bias reduction for all GLMs.
##  Methods for the detection of separation and infinite estimates in binomial-response models are provided by the 'detectseparation' R package (https://cran.r-project.org/package=detectseparation).
library(ggplot2)
library(pROC)
## Warning: package 'pROC' was built under R version 4.4.3
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
library(caret)
## Warning: package 'caret' was built under R version 4.4.3
## Loading required package: lattice
library(lattice)

# Set working directory to the location of the file
setwd("D:\\B\\St\\GAIN-master\\GAIN-master\\data")

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("heart_disease_uci.csv")

# Identify qualitative variables and factor them
qual_vars <- c("sex", "cp", "fbs", "restecg", "exang", "slope", "ca", "thal", "num","dataset")
data[qual_vars] <- lapply(data[qual_vars], as.factor)

# Handle missing values using missForest
imputed_data <- missForest(data)$ximp

Descriptive Plots

# Histogram of age
ggplot(imputed_data, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution", x = "Age", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Boxplot of cholesterol by sex
ggplot(imputed_data, aes(x = sex, y = chol, fill = sex)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Cholesterol by Sex", x = "Sex", y = "Cholesterol") +
  theme_minimal()

# Scatter plot of age vs trestbps
ggplot(imputed_data, aes(x = age, y = trestbps)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Age vs Resting Blood Pressure", x = "Age", y = "Trestbps") +
  theme_minimal()

Create Binary Target and More Descriptive Plots

# Create binary target for heart disease presence (num == "0" vs others)
imputed_data$diseased <- ifelse(imputed_data$num == "0", 0, 1)
imputed_data$diseased <- as.factor(imputed_data$diseased)

# Histogram of age by diseased
ggplot(imputed_data, aes(x = age, fill = diseased)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution by Disease", x = "Age", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Boxplot of chol by diseased
ggplot(imputed_data, aes(x = diseased, y = chol, fill = diseased)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Cholesterol by Disease", x = "Diseased", y = "Cholesterol") +
  theme_minimal()

Train/Test Split

# Split into train and test (90/10)
set.seed(123)
train_index <- sample(1:nrow(imputed_data), size = 0.9 * nrow(imputed_data))
train_data <- imputed_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- imputed_data[-train_index, ]

Univariate Logistic Regression Models

predictors <- c("age", "sex", "cp", "trestbps", "chol", "fbs", "restecg", "thalch", "exang", "oldpeak", "slope", "ca", "thal")

univariate_models <- list()
for (pred in predictors) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste("diseased ~", pred))
  model <- glm(formula, family = binomial(link = "logit"), data = train_data)
  univariate_models[[pred]] <- model
  cat("\nUnivariate model for", pred, ":\n")
  print(summary(model))
  preds <- predict(model, newdata = test_data, type = "response") > 0.5
  confuseMatrix <- table(preds, test_data$diseased)
  print(confuseMatrix)
  accuracy_binary <- sum(diag(confuseMatrix)) / sum(confuseMatrix)
  print(accuracy_binary)
  
  # Plot confusion matrix
  conf_df <- as.data.frame(confuseMatrix)
  colnames(conf_df) <- c("Prediction", "Reference", "Freq")
  print(ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
    geom_tile() +
    geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
    labs(title = paste("Confusion Matrix for Univariate Model:", pred)) +
    theme_minimal())
  
  # ROC curve if applicable
  roc_uni <- roc(test_data$diseased, predict(model, newdata = test_data, type = "response"))
  plot(roc_uni, main = paste("ROC for Univariate Model:", pred))
}
## 
## Univariate model for age :
## 
## Call:

Full Binary Model

cont_vars <- c("age", "trestbps", "chol", "thalch", "oldpeak")

full_binary_model <- glm(diseased ~ age + sex + cp + trestbps + chol + fbs + restecg + thalch + exang + oldpeak + slope + ca + thal,
                         family = binomial(link = "logit"), data = train_data)
summary(full_binary_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = diseased ~ age + sex + cp + trestbps + chol + fbs + 
##     restecg + thalch + exang + oldpeak + slope + ca + thal, family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
##     data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                           Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)              13.053809 535.413282   0.024 0.980549    
## age                      -0.048000   0.015436  -3.110 0.001873 ** 
## sexMale                   0.820773   0.304656   2.694 0.007058 ** 
## cpatypical angina        -1.996778   0.346862  -5.757 8.58e-09 ***
## cpnon-anginal            -1.554021   0.296233  -5.246 1.55e-07 ***
## cptypical angina         -1.230277   0.479494  -2.566 0.010294 *  
## trestbps                  0.014721   0.006292   2.340 0.019307 *  
## chol                     -0.003866   0.001232  -3.139 0.001697 ** 
## fbsTRUE                   0.364083   0.347468   1.048 0.294723    
## restecglv hypertrophy   -11.492272 535.411657  -0.021 0.982875    
## restecgnormal           -11.602409 535.411582  -0.022 0.982711    
## restecgst-t abnormality -11.943579 535.411635  -0.022 0.982203    
## thalch                   -0.010601   0.005694  -1.862 0.062624 .  
## exangTRUE                 0.924154   0.269057   3.435 0.000593 ***
## oldpeak                   0.349064   0.158915   2.197 0.028053 *  
## slopedownsloping         -0.293973   0.611567  -0.481 0.630739    
## slopeflat                 0.704910   0.366287   1.924 0.054295 .  
## slopeupsloping           -0.627969   0.397419  -1.580 0.114080    
## ca1                       3.241879   0.384056   8.441  < 2e-16 ***
## ca2                       4.083089   0.549144   7.435 1.04e-13 ***
## ca3                       2.554862   0.803736   3.179 0.001479 ** 
## thalfixed defect          0.524865   0.573270   0.916 0.359896    
## thalnormal               -0.642720   0.366700  -1.753 0.079651 .  
## thalreversable defect     0.742476   0.358780   2.069 0.038504 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1140.11  on 827  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  505.09  on 804  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 553.09
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 12
preds <- predict(full_binary_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response") > 0.5
confuseMatrix <- table(preds, test_data$diseased)
print(confuseMatrix)
##        
## preds    0  1
##   FALSE 35  6
##   TRUE   2 49
accuracy_binary <- sum(diag(confuseMatrix)) / sum(confuseMatrix)
print(accuracy_binary)
## [1] 0.9130435
conf_df <- as.data.frame(confuseMatrix)
colnames(conf_df) <- c("Prediction", "Reference", "Freq")
print(ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix for Full Binary Model") +
  theme_minimal())

roc_full <- roc(test_data$diseased, predict(full_binary_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response"))
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_full, main = "ROC for Full Binary Model")

Box-Tidwell Test and Transformations

bt_data <- train_data
bt_data$oldpeak_shifted <- bt_data$oldpeak + 3
write.csv(imputed_data, "imputed_data1.csv", row.names = FALSE)
bt_data <- bt_data[bt_data$age > 0 & bt_data$trestbps > 0 & bt_data$chol > 0 & bt_data$thalch > 0 & bt_data$oldpeak_shifted > 0, ]

bt_result <- boxTidwell(as.numeric(diseased) - 1 ~ age + trestbps + chol + thalch + oldpeak_shifted,
                        ~ sex + cp + fbs + restecg + exang + slope + ca + thal,
                        data = bt_data)
print(bt_result)
##                 MLE of lambda Score Statistic (t) Pr(>|t|)
## age                  -1.48624              0.6039   0.5461
## trestbps              2.40354              0.3118   0.7553
## chol                  1.71311              0.0605   0.9518
## thalch                6.94920             -0.5306   0.5958
## oldpeak_shifted       0.48988             -0.2359   0.8136
## 
## iterations =  15 
## 
## Score test for null hypothesis that all lambdas = 1:
## F = 0.1552, df = 5 and 651, Pr(>F) = 0.9785
powers <- bt_result$result[, "MLE of lambda"]
names(powers) <- rownames(bt_result$result)
for (var in names(powers)){
  print(paste("Variable:", var, "Power:", powers[var]))
}
## [1] "Variable: age Power: -1.48623658440669"
## [1] "Variable: trestbps Power: 2.4035384262381"
## [1] "Variable: chol Power: 1.71310626157575"
## [1] "Variable: thalch Power: 6.94920472250204"
## [1] "Variable: oldpeak_shifted Power: 0.489881690404146"
train_data$oldpeak_shifted <- train_data$oldpeak + 3
test_data$oldpeak_shifted <- test_data$oldpeak + 3
for (var in names(powers)) {
  if (var == "oldpeak_shifted") {
    train_data$oldpeak_trans <- (train_data[[var]]) ^ powers[var]
    test_data$oldpeak_trans <- (test_data[[var]]) ^ powers[var]
  } else {
    train_data[[paste0(var, "_trans")]] <- train_data[[var]] ^ powers[var]
    test_data[[paste0(var, "_trans")]] <- test_data[[var]] ^ powers[var]
  }
}

write.csv(imputed_data, "imputed_data_transformed.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Transformed Full Binary Model

full_binary_model_trans <- glm(diseased ~ age_trans + sex + cp + trestbps_trans + chol_trans + fbs + restecg + thalch_trans + exang + oldpeak_trans + slope + ca + thal,
                               family = binomial(link = "logit"), data = train_data)
summary(full_binary_model_trans)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = diseased ~ age_trans + sex + cp + trestbps_trans + 
##     chol_trans + fbs + restecg + thalch_trans + exang + oldpeak_trans + 
##     slope + ca + thal, family = binomial(link = "logit"), data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                           Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)              6.318e+00  5.354e+02   0.012  0.99058    
## age_trans                5.062e+02  1.565e+02   3.235  0.00122 ** 
## sexMale                  9.308e-01  3.061e-01   3.041  0.00236 ** 
## cpatypical angina       -2.121e+00  3.456e-01  -6.136 8.44e-10 ***
## cpnon-anginal           -1.566e+00  2.930e-01  -5.343 9.12e-08 ***
## cptypical angina        -1.252e+00  4.754e-01  -2.633  0.00845 ** 
## trestbps_trans           6.578e-06  2.685e-06   2.450  0.01430 *  
## chol_trans              -2.923e-05  1.729e-05  -1.690  0.09094 .  
## fbsTRUE                  3.359e-01  3.484e-01   0.964  0.33503    
## restecglv hypertrophy   -1.151e+01  5.354e+02  -0.021  0.98285    
## restecgnormal           -1.148e+01  5.354e+02  -0.021  0.98289    
## restecgst-t abnormality -1.174e+01  5.354e+02  -0.022  0.98251    
## thalch_trans            -2.056e-16  9.971e-17  -2.062  0.03919 *  
## exangTRUE                8.754e-01  2.677e-01   3.270  0.00108 ** 
## oldpeak_trans            1.126e+00  6.518e-01   1.728  0.08406 .  
## slopedownsloping         7.259e-02  6.044e-01   0.120  0.90441    
## slopeflat                8.179e-01  3.639e-01   2.247  0.02462 *  
## slopeupsloping          -4.635e-01  3.912e-01  -1.185  0.23604    
## ca1                      3.226e+00  3.757e-01   8.586  < 2e-16 ***
## ca2                      4.017e+00  5.223e-01   7.691 1.46e-14 ***
## ca3                      2.358e+00  8.082e-01   2.917  0.00353 ** 
## thalfixed defect         4.648e-01  5.701e-01   0.815  0.41488    
## thalnormal              -6.524e-01  3.642e-01  -1.791  0.07327 .  
## thalreversable defect    7.550e-01  3.551e-01   2.126  0.03348 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1140.11  on 827  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  512.76  on 804  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 560.76
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 12
preds <- predict(full_binary_model_trans, newdata = test_data, type = "response") > 0.5
confuseMatrix <- table(preds, test_data$diseased)
print(confuseMatrix)
##        
## preds    0  1
##   FALSE 35  5
##   TRUE   2 50
accuracy_binary <- sum(diag(confuseMatrix)) / sum(confuseMatrix)
print(accuracy_binary)
## [1] 0.923913
conf_df <- as.data.frame(confuseMatrix)
colnames(conf_df) <- c("Prediction", "Reference", "Freq")
print(ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix for Transformed Full Binary Model") +
  theme_minimal())

roc_trans <- roc(test_data$diseased, predict(full_binary_model_trans, newdata = test_data, type = "response"))
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_trans, main = "ROC for Transformed Full Binary Model")

Stepwise AIC Binary Model

step_binary_model <- stepAIC(full_binary_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE)
summary(step_binary_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = diseased ~ age + sex + cp + trestbps + chol + thalch + 
##     exang + oldpeak + slope + ca + thal, family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
##     data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                        Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            1.192125   1.354480   0.880  0.37879    
## age                   -0.046755   0.015059  -3.105  0.00190 ** 
## sexMale                0.851656   0.303014   2.811  0.00494 ** 
## cpatypical angina     -1.976254   0.345005  -5.728 1.02e-08 ***
## cpnon-anginal         -1.533103   0.295214  -5.193 2.07e-07 ***
## cptypical angina      -1.159376   0.472066  -2.456  0.01405 *  
## trestbps               0.014425   0.006231   2.315  0.02060 *  
## chol                  -0.003433   0.001187  -2.891  0.00383 ** 
## thalch                -0.009982   0.005679  -1.758  0.07881 .  
## exangTRUE              0.918111   0.269241   3.410  0.00065 ***
## oldpeak                0.335857   0.158966   2.113  0.03462 *  
## slopedownsloping      -0.245046   0.607993  -0.403  0.68692    
## slopeflat              0.701596   0.364235   1.926  0.05408 .  
## slopeupsloping        -0.622834   0.394141  -1.580  0.11405    
## ca1                    3.222034   0.380123   8.476  < 2e-16 ***
## ca2                    4.104860   0.542946   7.560 4.02e-14 ***
## ca3                    2.608689   0.811267   3.216  0.00130 ** 
## thalfixed defect       0.567360   0.577250   0.983  0.32567    
## thalnormal            -0.561345   0.351027  -1.599  0.10979    
## thalreversable defect  0.842689   0.343047   2.456  0.01403 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1140.11  on 827  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  507.58  on 808  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 547.58
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 6
preds <- predict(step_binary_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response") > 0.5
step_confuseMatrix <- table(preds, test_data$diseased)
print(step_confuseMatrix)
##        
## preds    0  1
##   FALSE 35  6
##   TRUE   2 49
accuracy_binary <- sum(diag(step_confuseMatrix)) / sum(step_confuseMatrix)
print(accuracy_binary)
## [1] 0.9130435
conf_df <- as.data.frame(step_confuseMatrix)
colnames(conf_df) <- c("Prediction", "Reference", "Freq")
print(ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix for Stepwise AIC Binary Model") +
  theme_minimal())

roc_step_aic <- roc(test_data$diseased, predict(step_binary_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response"))
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_step_aic, main = "ROC for Stepwise AIC Binary Model")

Stepwise BIC Binary Model

step_binary_model_bic <- stepAIC(full_binary_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE, k = log(nrow(train_data)))
summary(step_binary_model_bic)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = diseased ~ sex + cp + chol + exang + oldpeak + 
##     slope + ca, family = binomial(link = "logit"), data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)       -0.786957   0.425182  -1.851  0.06419 .  
## sexMale            1.191254   0.283971   4.195 2.73e-05 ***
## cpatypical angina -2.015544   0.330895  -6.091 1.12e-09 ***
## cpnon-anginal     -1.653018   0.280015  -5.903 3.56e-09 ***
## cptypical angina  -1.215766   0.466186  -2.608  0.00911 ** 
## chol              -0.003472   0.001114  -3.116  0.00183 ** 
## exangTRUE          1.121567   0.251267   4.464 8.06e-06 ***
## oldpeak            0.386283   0.145972   2.646  0.00814 ** 
## slopedownsloping  -0.477046   0.582556  -0.819  0.41285    
## slopeflat          0.578273   0.319286   1.811  0.07012 .  
## slopeupsloping    -0.893934   0.319896  -2.794  0.00520 ** 
## ca1                2.856216   0.346536   8.242  < 2e-16 ***
## ca2                3.473541   0.475803   7.300 2.87e-13 ***
## ca3                2.340425   0.724746   3.229  0.00124 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1140.11  on 827  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  536.92  on 814  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 564.92
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 6
preds <- predict(step_binary_model_bic, newdata = test_data, type = "response") > 0.5
BIC_confuseMatrix <- table(preds, test_data$diseased)
print(BIC_confuseMatrix)
##        
## preds    0  1
##   FALSE 33  7
##   TRUE   4 48
BIC_accuracy_binary <- sum(diag(BIC_confuseMatrix)) / sum(BIC_confuseMatrix)
print(BIC_accuracy_binary)
## [1] 0.8804348
conf_df <- as.data.frame(BIC_confuseMatrix)
colnames(conf_df) <- c("Prediction", "Reference", "Freq")
print(ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix for Stepwise BIC Binary Model") +
  theme_minimal())

roc_step_bic <- roc(test_data$diseased, predict(step_binary_model_bic, newdata = test_data, type = "response"))
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_step_bic, main = "ROC for Stepwise BIC Binary Model")

Ordinal Logistic Regression Preparation

diseased_subset_train <- train_data[train_data$num != "0", ]
diseased_subset_test <- test_data[test_data$num != "0", ]

diseased_subset_train$num <- factor(diseased_subset_train$num, levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4"), ordered = TRUE)
diseased_subset_test$num <- factor(diseased_subset_test$num, levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4"), ordered = TRUE)
write.csv(rbind(diseased_subset_train, diseased_subset_test), "diseased_subset.csv", row.names = FALSE)
diseased_subset_train <- droplevels(diseased_subset_train)
diseased_subset_test <- droplevels(diseased_subset_test)

diseased_subset_train <- diseased_subset_train[, !colnames(diseased_subset_train) %in% c("diseased", "oldpeak_shifted", "age_trans", "trestbps_trans", "chol_trans", "thalch_trans", "oldpeak_trans")]
diseased_subset_test <- diseased_subset_test[, !colnames(diseased_subset_test) %in% c("diseased", "oldpeak_shifted", "age_trans", "trestbps_trans", "chol_trans", "thalch_trans", "oldpeak_trans")]

Full Ordinal Model

full_ordinal_model <- polr(num ~ ., data = diseased_subset_train, Hess = TRUE)
summary(full_ordinal_model)
## Call:
## polr(formula = num ~ ., data = diseased_subset_train, Hess = TRUE)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                              Value Std. Error    t value
## id                      -2.599e-03  6.821e-04 -3.810e+00
## age                     -2.875e-02  1.520e-02 -1.892e+00
## sexMale                  5.414e-01  3.714e-01  1.458e+00
## datasetHungary          -1.825e+01  7.345e-08 -2.485e+08
## datasetSwitzerland       1.141e+00  2.420e-01  4.718e+00
## datasetVA Long Beach     8.311e-01  1.947e-01  4.269e+00
## cpatypical angina       -8.593e-01  6.512e-01 -1.320e+00
## cpnon-anginal            2.754e-01  2.999e-01  9.182e-01
## cptypical angina        -8.606e-03  5.225e-01 -1.647e-02
## trestbps                -5.923e-04  5.921e-03 -1.000e-01
## chol                     4.553e-04  1.347e-03  3.380e-01
## fbsTRUE                  1.675e-01  2.910e-01  5.757e-01
## restecglv hypertrophy   -2.395e-01  1.974e-01 -1.213e+00
## restecgnormal           -3.343e-01  1.476e-01 -2.266e+00
## restecgst-t abnormality  2.690e-01  1.809e-01  1.487e+00
## thalch                  -1.712e-02  4.349e-03 -3.938e+00
## exangTRUE                3.038e-01  2.428e-01  1.251e+00
## oldpeak                  5.576e-01  1.094e-01  5.098e+00
## slopedownsloping        -4.673e-01  4.211e-01 -1.110e+00
## slopeflat               -5.829e-01  3.272e-01 -1.782e+00
## slopeupsloping          -6.187e-01  3.930e-01 -1.574e+00
## ca1                      3.605e-01  3.201e-01  1.126e+00
## ca2                      1.628e+00  3.631e-01  4.482e+00
## ca3                      1.711e+00  5.506e-01  3.107e+00
## thalfixed defect         2.803e-01  4.253e-01  6.591e-01
## thalnormal              -3.519e-01  4.276e-01 -8.230e-01
## thalreversable defect   -5.086e-02  3.305e-01 -1.539e-01
## 
## Intercepts:
##     Value         Std. Error    t value      
## 1|2 -3.682200e+00  8.380000e-02 -4.396030e+01
## 2|3 -2.231400e+00  1.600000e-01 -1.394900e+01
## 3|4 -3.150000e-02  2.646000e-01 -1.190000e-01
## 
## Residual Deviance: 793.6695 
## AIC: 853.6695

Stepwise AIC Ordinal Model

step_ordinal_model <- stepAIC(full_ordinal_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE)
preds_class <- predict(step_ordinal_model, newdata = diseased_subset_test, type = "class")
AIC_ordered_confuseMatrix <- table(preds_class, diseased_subset_test$num)
print(AIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)
##            
## preds_class  1  2  3  4
##           1 23  6  7  1
##           2  2  1  3  0
##           3  3  4  4  1
##           4  0  0  0  0
accuracy <- sum(diag(AIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)) / sum(AIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)
print(accuracy)
## [1] 0.5090909
conf_df <- as.data.frame(AIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)
colnames(conf_df) <- c("Prediction", "Reference", "Freq")
print(ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix for Stepwise AIC Ordinal Model") +
  theme_minimal())

print(ggplot() +
  geom_bar(data = data.frame(pred = preds_class), aes(x = pred, fill = "Predicted")) +
  geom_bar(data = data.frame(actual = diseased_subset_test$num), aes(x = actual, fill = "Actual"), alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Predicted vs Actual for AIC Ordinal Model") +
  theme_minimal())

Stepwise BIC Ordinal Model

step_ordinal_model_bic <- stepAIC(full_ordinal_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE, k = log(nrow(diseased_subset_train)))
summary(step_ordinal_model_bic)
## Call:
## polr(formula = num ~ dataset + thalch + oldpeak + ca, data = diseased_subset_train, 
##     Hess = TRUE)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                          Value Std. Error    t value
## datasetHungary       -18.02092  2.454e-07 -7.343e+07
## datasetSwitzerland    -0.02650  2.861e-01 -9.261e-02
## datasetVA Long Beach  -0.18405  2.593e-01 -7.097e-01
## thalch                -0.01672  4.677e-03 -3.574e+00
## oldpeak                0.52719  9.089e-02  5.800e+00
## ca1                    0.28090  2.763e-01  1.017e+00
## ca2                    1.18288  2.897e-01  4.083e+00
## ca3                    1.39705  5.130e-01  2.723e+00
## 
## Intercepts:
##     Value         Std. Error    t value      
## 1|2 -1.518100e+00  7.087000e-01 -2.142100e+00
## 2|3 -1.452000e-01  7.024000e-01 -2.067000e-01
## 3|4  1.974100e+00  7.184000e-01  2.748100e+00
## 
## Residual Deviance: 818.3305 
## AIC: 840.3305
preds_class_bic <- predict(step_ordinal_model_bic, newdata = diseased_subset_test, type = "class")
BIC_ordered_confuseMatrix <- table(preds_class_bic, diseased_subset_test$num)
print(BIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)
##                
## preds_class_bic  1  2  3  4
##               1 23  5  7  1
##               2  2  3  2  0
##               3  3  3  5  1
##               4  0  0  0  0
BIC_accuracy <- sum(diag(BIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)) / sum(BIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)
print(BIC_accuracy)
## [1] 0.5636364
conf_df <- as.data.frame(BIC_ordered_confuseMatrix)
colnames(conf_df) <- c("Prediction", "Reference", "Freq")
print(ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix for Stepwise BIC Ordinal Model") +
  theme_minimal())

print(ggplot() +
  geom_bar(data = data.frame(pred = preds_class_bic), aes(x = pred, fill = "Predicted")) +
  geom_bar(data = data.frame(actual = diseased_subset_test$num), aes(x = actual, fill = "Actual"), alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Predicted vs Actual for BIC Ordinal Model") +
  theme_minimal())

summary(step_ordinal_model)
## Call:
## polr(formula = num ~ age + sex + dataset + restecg + thalch + 
##     oldpeak + ca, data = diseased_subset_train, Hess = TRUE)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                             Value Std. Error    t value
## age                      -0.03385  1.718e-02 -1.970e+00
## sexMale                   0.52864  3.762e-01  1.405e+00
## datasetHungary          -18.31863  7.128e-07 -2.570e+07
## datasetSwitzerland       -0.20633  3.258e-01 -6.333e-01
## datasetVA Long Beach     -0.42000  3.097e-01 -1.356e+00
## restecglv hypertrophy    -0.20596  3.694e-01 -5.576e-01
## restecgnormal            -0.38145  3.366e-01 -1.133e+00
## restecgst-t abnormality   0.27744  3.919e-01  7.079e-01
## thalch                   -0.01887  4.860e-03 -3.884e+00
## oldpeak                   0.54491  9.240e-02  5.897e+00
## ca1                       0.37669  3.083e-01  1.222e+00
## ca2                       1.53381  3.680e-01  4.168e+00
## ca3                       1.65453  5.508e-01  3.004e+00
## 
## Intercepts:
##     Value         Std. Error    t value      
## 1|2 -3.395900e+00  9.751000e-01 -3.482500e+00
## 2|3 -1.986900e+00  9.675000e-01 -2.053600e+00
## 3|4  1.760000e-01  9.715000e-01  1.812000e-01
## 
## Residual Deviance: 806.3997 
## AIC: 838.3997

Model Evaluation

preds_prob_binary <- predict(step_binary_model_bic, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
roc_binary <- pROC::roc(as.numeric(test_data$diseased), preds_prob_binary)
## Setting levels: control = 1, case = 2
## Setting direction: controls < cases
print(roc_binary)
## 
## Call:
## roc.default(response = as.numeric(test_data$diseased), predictor = preds_prob_binary)
## 
## Data: preds_prob_binary in 37 controls (as.numeric(test_data$diseased) 1) < 55 cases (as.numeric(test_data$diseased) 2).
## Area under the curve: 0.9568
cat("AUC for binary model:", auc(roc_binary), "\n")
## AUC for binary model: 0.9567568
plot(roc_binary, main = "ROC for Selected Binary Model")

binary_conf <- confusionMatrix(factor(ifelse(preds_prob_binary > 0.5, 1, 0), levels = c(0,1)), test_data$diseased)
print(binary_conf)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 33  7
##          1  4 48
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.8804          
##                  95% CI : (0.7961, 0.9388)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5978          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 2.374e-09       
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.7546          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.5465          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.8919          
##             Specificity : 0.8727          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.8250          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.9231          
##              Prevalence : 0.4022          
##          Detection Rate : 0.3587          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.4348          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.8823          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : 0               
## 
precision_binary <- binary_conf$byClass['Precision']
recall_binary <- binary_conf$byClass['Recall']
f1_binary <- binary_conf$byClass['F1']
cat("Precision:", precision_binary, "\nRecall:", recall_binary, "\nF1-Score:", f1_binary, "\n")
## Precision: 0.825 
## Recall: 0.8918919 
## F1-Score: 0.8571429
plot(table(binary_conf$table), main = "Confusion Matrix Plot from Caret")

preds_ordinal_class <- predict(step_ordinal_model, newdata = diseased_subset_test, type = "class")

ordinal_conf <- confusionMatrix(preds_ordinal_class, diseased_subset_test$num)
print(ordinal_conf)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  1  2  3  4
##          1 23  6  7  1
##          2  2  1  3  0
##          3  3  4  4  1
##          4  0  0  0  0
## 
## Overall Statistics
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.5091          
##                  95% CI : (0.3707, 0.6465)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5091          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.5539          
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.1538          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : NA              
## 
## Statistics by Class:
## 
##                      Class: 1 Class: 2 Class: 3 Class: 4
## Sensitivity            0.8214  0.09091  0.28571  0.00000
## Specificity            0.4815  0.88636  0.80488  1.00000
## Pos Pred Value         0.6216  0.16667  0.33333      NaN
## Neg Pred Value         0.7222  0.79592  0.76744  0.96364
## Prevalence             0.5091  0.20000  0.25455  0.03636
## Detection Rate         0.4182  0.01818  0.07273  0.00000
## Detection Prevalence   0.6727  0.10909  0.21818  0.00000
## Balanced Accuracy      0.6515  0.48864  0.54530  0.50000
weighted_precision_ordinal <- ordinal_conf$byClass[, 'Precision'] %*% table(diseased_subset_test$num) / length(diseased_subset_test$num)
weighted_recall_ordinal <- ordinal_conf$byClass[, 'Recall'] %*% table(diseased_subset_test$num) / length(diseased_subset_test$num)
weighted_f1_ordinal <- ordinal_conf$byClass[, 'F1'] %*% table(diseased_subset_test$num) / length(diseased_subset_test$num)
cat("Weighted Precision:", weighted_precision_ordinal, "\nWeighted Recall:", weighted_recall_ordinal, "\nWeighted F1-Score:", weighted_f1_ordinal, "\n")
## Weighted Precision: NA 
## Weighted Recall: 0.5090909 
## Weighted F1-Score: NA
cat("Cohen's Kappa for ordinal model:", ordinal_conf$overall['Kappa'], "\n")
## Cohen's Kappa for ordinal model: 0.1538462
plot(table(ordinal_conf$table), main = "Ordinal Confusion Matrix Plot from Caret")
